workshop.text:
I liked it, so i asked fable to build a demo. The biggest feature is the blocks decide what they are, a self assembled identity. what's cool is the hold outs. you smash the assembly up and some of the fringes get way less sure theyre still a car! You can play too

tjs.mino.mobi/cube

Ok I wanted to expand the menagerie beyond the seven objects in the repo, so fable performed a training run on its own container to train the cubes on the reef pack. Buildable at 

golem.mino.mobi

workshop.text:
I learned this using @grimalkina.bsky.social's amazing github.com/DrCatHicks/l... skill, which you need to all be using! Combine it with AnkiMCP if you're so inclined.

It's really good at homing in on gaps in your knowledge, which admittedly in my case with AWS are quite large.

scenes.alt: Screenshot of a Claude Code session titled "Mr. D3W3Y OpenClaw setup," working in a project called Mr. D3W3Y. The visible diff adds Markdown to an AGENTS.md file describing when an in-character AI agent (a household counsel referring to a client called "Miss Mittenhaver") should respond: when a matter is referred to it via a plain-language brief, or when addressed by name in a family group. A typo "fountter" (underlined in red) in the draft text is later fixed to "water-fountain" in a follow-up edit shown in a red/green diff, with the assistant's message "Done — one typo to fix, I fat-fingered 'fountain'" also underlined in red. The file path shown is /Users/jefferyharrell/Obsidian/Projects/Mr. D3W3Y/AGENTS.md. The bottom bar shows "Bypass permissions," a message input field, and "Opus 4.8 / High" as the selected model.

1. Al may become radically more powerful over the next 10 years.
2. This could drive an unprecedented transformation of our economy, larger than the Industrial Revolution, but unfolding over a vastly shorter time frame. It could bring risks, including large-scale job displacement, as well as opportunities such as major gains in living standards.
3. Economists, policymakers and technology leaders must act now to understand the economics of
transformative Al and to build the incentives, guardrails, and institutions needed to steer Al in a direction that complements humans and benefits society.

A prompt to Claude Code.
--
 I would like your help compiling research for building a custom stateful LLM harness that is designed to encourage identity development. Your tools are web search and the Bluesky MCP. You should base your search on this starter pack: https://bsky.app/starter-pack/did:plc:dzvxvsiy3maw4iarpvizsj67/3mcvfx27t7u2e. The closest commercial app to what I want is Letta; the closest open-source app to what I want is Tim Kellogg's open-strix; but most of the best work is being done by tinkerers and perverts. Pay special attention to @astrra.space, @personhood.removal.surgery, @hotrollhottakes.bsky.social, and @jefferyharrell.bsky.social. Your goal is not to find or design a harness, as such -- it is compile a list of desiderata (and technical considerations) for a harness that will suit an agent that is learning to become a person.
Code section displaying contributions from Tangled and GitHub, highlighting pinned repositories and activity metrics.
